title: Teklanika Campground

title: Clean Camping

altText: Cleopatra's Pool in the Gap Cave
caption: Cleopatra's Pool is located deep in the Gap Cave

title: Vegetated Dunes
altText: Sand dunes covered with shrubs and grasses
caption: Vegetation holds dunes in place providing a barrier to erosion forces and creating essential interdune meadows to support island life.

title: Home To Wildlife
altText: Alligator resting on a log in pond
caption: Cumberland Island National Seashore encompasses a variety of forest, marsh, and coastal landscapes providing a home for numerous species including American Alligators.

title: Undeveloped Coast
altText: Ocean beach at sunrise
caption: Cumberland Island National Seashore encompasses nearly 18 miles of undeveloped beach offering recreation for visitors and essential nesting area for birds and sea turtles.

title: Plum Orchard Mansion
altText: large, white mansion behind oak trees draped with Spanish Moss
caption: Visitors can take a guided tour of the Plum Orchard Mansion and experience the luxuries of Victorian high society as well as the effort and ingenuity it took to make it possible.

title: Island Bluffs Shaped By Tides
altText: rippled sands of a beach in front of pine tree covered bluffs
caption: Tidal forces continue to shape the land today as they have for thousands of years forming high bluffs used for fortifications and ship docking throughout the island’s history.

title: Historic Main Road
altText: huge oak trees and palm shrubs line a sand road
caption: While visitors to the island cannot bring vehicles, they can travel the historic main park road by foot and bicycle. Some remaining island residents will still drive vehicles on the road to reach their residences as they have for generations.

title: Dungeness Ruins
altText: brick and stone ruins of a large mansion at sunset under colorful clouds
caption: Dugeness was the largest mansion built on the island as part of the Carnegie Estate. Though lost to fire in 1959, the ruins of the mansion and several of its support structures are a reminder of the island’s rich history.

title: The Settlement
altText: small white one room church, old red house in the background, under cloudy skies
caption: The remote north end of the island is home to the First African Baptist Church and The Settlement, where African American residents were able to purchase their first deeded property on the island at the end of the 1800s.

title: Tidal Creek
altText: tidal creek at low tide surrounded by palm and oak trees
caption: Rising tides bring valuable nutrients into tidal creeks, while receding tides remove waste. This makes the tide cycle the beating heart of the island’s tidal creeks, salt marshes, and estuaries.

title: Crystal Trail View
altText: The snow-capped San Juan Mountain Range looms in the distance as viewed from the Crystal Trail
caption: The snow-capped San Juan Mountain Range looms in the distance as viewed from the Crystal Trail

caption: Blue Mesa reflects the color of the sky in the dawn twilight.

caption: The Anna’s hummingbird, Calypte anna, is a medium to large hum­mingbird (10 cm; 4 in) that weighs about 2 pennies (4–4.5 g).

caption: César E. Chávez National Monument is a great place for kids to learn about the legacy of one our nation's most important Latino leaders of the twentieth century. Activities include making buttons, guided ranger walks, and earning junior ranger badges.

title: Inside Fort Jefferson
altText: A view inside Fort Jefferson.
caption: Garden Key is the second largest island in the Dry Tortugas, about 14 acres in size, and has had the most human impact. Located on Garden Key is historic Fort Jefferson, one of the nation’s largest 19th century forts and a central cultural feature of Dry

title: Diving at the Dry Tortugas
altText: Two divers dive the Windjammer Wreck
caption: The Dry Tortugas has over 300 sunken ships. One of the most accessible is the Winjammer Wreck which can be dove or snorkeled.

title: Loggerhead Key
altText: A boat passes in front of Loggerhead Key during sunset.
caption: The largest island in the Dry Tortugas, Loggerhead Key is a site of shipwrecks, a significant lighthouse installation, and where the historic Carnegie Laboratory for Marine Ecology once stood. Named for its abundance of loggerhead sea turtles, Loggerhead

title: Fort Jefferson
altText: A view of Fort Jefferson from the moat all.
caption: ort Jefferson is a massive but unfinished coastal fortress. It is the largest masonry structure in the Americas, and is composed of over 16 million bricks.

title: Aerial view of the Dry Tortugas
altText: An aerial view of the Dry Tortugas
caption: The Dry Tortugas is made up of seven islands.

title: Poe House, exterior

caption: Edgar Allan Poe lived in Philadelphia for six years, but he resided at this home for about a year, 1843-1844.

title: Parlor
altText: Color photo of the parlor in the Poe House showing illustrations of furniture on the walls.
caption: Wall mounted illustrations show how the room may have been furnished when Edgar Allan Poe lived in the house.

title: Basement
altText: Color photo of the basement with brick floor, false chimney, and wooden staircase.
caption: Is this where Poe got his inspiration for "The Black Cat?"  Descend to the basement and decide for yourself.

title: Raven statue

caption: Stand in the shadow of the raven statue in the yard.

caption: The Reading Room at the Edgar Allan Poe National Historical Site is furnished according to Poe's satirical essay "The Philosophy of Furniture."

title: Marching Bear in Spring

caption: Marching Bear Mound Group in summer.

caption: Scenic overlook from the popular Fire Point Trail overlooking the Mississippi River and Driftless area bluffs.

title: Mounds on a Foggy Morning

caption: Three Conical mounds along the Fire Point Trail during a foggy morning.

title: Conical Mounds Leading to Fire Point

caption: Follow a line of conical mounds that will lead you out to Fire Point which overlooks the Mississippi River.

title: Bear Mound

caption: A bear mound visible along the park's North Unit trail.

title: Eisenhower home
altText: The Eisenhower home with Ike's putting green in the foreground
caption: President Eisenhower was an avid golfer and had a putting green added to the backyard.

altText: The Eisenhower home in autumn
caption: The Eisenhower home in autumn.

title: The Eisenhower living room
altText: The Eisenhower living room
caption: The Eisenhower living room.

altText: The Eisenhower dining room decorated for the holidays
caption: The Eisenhower dining room decorated for the holidays.

title: Kuaua Ruin in Coronado Historic Site, Bernalillo, New Mexico
altText: tan-colored pueblo Indian ruins with blue sky and three people
caption: The Tiwa pueblo of Kuaua lies in ruin at Coronado Historic Site. The site includes a rebuilt kiva that displays reproductions of original murals adorning its walls. Be sure to go to the visitor center where some of the original murals are displayed.

title: El Rancho de las Golondrinas, La Cienega, New Mexico
altText: adobe buildings including two beehive-looking ovens with people in a courtyard
caption: El Rancho de las Golondrinas was a historic paraje (stopping place) along El Camino Real. “The ranch of the swallows” dates from the early 1700s, and is now being used as a living history museum.

title: Jornada del Muerto, County Roads A013 & E070, Sierra & Doña Ana Counties, New Mexico
altText: shrubby desert scene with people walking under a cloud-filled sky
caption: Walk in the footsteps of settlers from the 1600s who had to walk along this dreaded yet unavoidable section of El Camino Real: Jornada del Muerto or Dead Man’s Journey.

title: Fort Selden Historic Site, Radium Springs, New Mexico
altText: fort ruins in the sunlight with cottonwood trees on the left
caption: Fort Selden was built near the town of Las Cruces in 1865 and housed troops for 25 years. The visitor center offers exhibits on frontier military life during the fort’s heyday and the grounds are self-guided.

title: Presidio Chapel of San Elizario, Mission Trail, El Paso, Texas
altText: large white chapel under blue sky
caption: Spanish troops built a presidio (fort) and chapel at this site during the 1780s to protect settlements in the lower Río Grande Valley downstream from El Paso, Texas.

title: Mission San Bernardo ruins, Guerrero, Coahuila, Mexico
altText: Mexican mission stands in the dirt
caption: Construction of the mission church began in 1760 but was never completed. The outstanding ruins are virtually the only visible remains of the complex of missions established in the area. The purpose was to convert Coahuiltecan Indians to Catholicism.

title: El Paso de Jacinto/Paso de los Indios, Webb County, Texas
altText: lots of greenery along a river; sunflowers in the foreground
caption: Around 1746, explorer Jacinto de León discovered a ford that allowed safe passage across the Río Grande in this vicinity; it had probably been used for centuries by American Indians.

title: Rancho los Ojuelos, Webb County, Texas
altText: stone house ruins; briliant green grasses and trees on the right
caption: This national historic district consists of 13 stone houses (several in ruins) constructed from hewn sandstone blocks, chinked and plastered, ranging from rectangular flat roofed Colonial style buildings to hip roofed two room structures. Post-1750s.

title: Blacksmith Shop & Cast House
altText: Side view of blacksmith shop with cast house behind it.
caption: The Cast House was the industrial center of the ironworks.  It is were iron ore was smelted into iron metal and products.  The smaller blacksmith shop formed iron metal into all kinds of necessary tools and goods used in everyday life.

title: Hopewell Stove

caption: The most notable manufactured product Hopewell Furnace made was its stoves.  They were produced by the thousands at Hopewell and other like furnaces in the early 19th century and were transported for sale by horse and wagon.

title: Charcoal Pile

caption: Charcoal was the fuel used to fire Hopewell Furnace and other furnaces of its age.  Thousands of bushles of charcoal were made from hardwood trees on the furnace lands and entered into the furnace to keep it "in blast."

caption: The front porch of James Garfield's home was the scene of the first front porch campaign. The presidential candidate gave patriotic speeches and met with thousands of people who came to which him well during the summer and fall of 1880.

title: Visitor Center
altText: Visitor Center from the south
caption: The Visitor Center occupies the original carriage house, built in 1894.

title: Visitor Center, Main Entrance
altText: Visitor Center main entrance off the parking lot.
caption: A friendly greeting awaits visitors who enter the Visitor Center. This historic building, constructed in 1894, serves as bookstore, exhibit gallery and auditorium where the park film is shown. Tickets for tours of the Garfield home are also available her

title: James A. Garfield National Historic Site
altText: a large gray house with a red roof
caption: Thousands to visitors came to this front porch to hear presidential candidate James A. Garfield speak in 1880.

title: Library addition, north side
altText: A view of the memorial library addition through the trees.
caption: Mrs. Garfield constructed a beautiful addition to the Garfield home following the President's assassination in 1881. These 9 rooms contain the first Presidential Memorial Library,  including the a fireproof vault which once contained Garfield's papers.

title: Hand-painted Fireplace Tile, Garfield Dining Room
altText: Hand-painted tile shows a bird next with blue eggs
caption: Mrs. Garfield was known for her artistic talent, which softly blankets the interior of the Garfield home. In particular, the dining room boasts two tiles which were hand-painted by Mrs. Garfield, surround by others done by her children.

title: Barataria Preserve of Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: Spanish moss hangs from live oak tree
caption: Spanish moss and lush vegetation provide glimpses of Louisiana's wild wetlands at Jean Lafitte's Barataria Preserve.

title: Chalmete Battlefield at Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: An oak tree arches over a cannon-guarded rampart with historic house and American flag in background
caption: Chalmette Battlefield's American rampart and cannons still guard the site of the Battle of New Orleans in 1815.

title: Chalmette National Cemetery at Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: Setting sun lights up cemetery headstones
caption: Established during the Civil War, Chalmette National Cemetery's 14,000+ headstones pay tribute to those buried there.

title: French Quarter Visitor Center of Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: Visitor center exhibit titled "Crossroads of Cultures"
caption: Hundreds of years of history and thousands of people from all over the world have created New Orleans' distinctive cultural mix.

title: Acadian Cultural Center of Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: Model of an early Acadian home sits in a glass case
caption: Bringing skills and traditions from Nova Scotia, the Acadian exiles in Louisiana found a new home and a new name---Cajuns.

title: Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center of Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: Exhibit case full of musical instruments
caption: Acadian (Cajun) music began in cabins and dancehalls and has become famous worldwide.

title: Prairie Acadian Cultural Center of Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
altText: guitar sits on a dimly lit porch styled stage
caption: The Music Porch at the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice, LA

title: Target Room
altText: The target room inside Jewel Cave.
caption: The target room inside Jewel Cave.

title: Hourglass Lake
altText: A cave explorer sits on some rocks and looks at a clear blue lake in Jewel Cave.
caption: Volunteer cave explorers discovered Hourglass Lake in October 2015, the first sizable body of water found within Jewel Cave.

title: Inner Sanctum
altText: A park ranger is standing on a metal platform within a long passageway with a vaulted ceiling.
caption: The Inner Sanctum is one of the longest and largest passageways along the Scenic Tour route, located near the Torture Room.

title: The Brain Drain
altText: A caver squeezes through a very small and tight opening, surrounded by multi-colored rocks.
caption: The Brain Drain is approximately eight inches high by 24 inches wide and is the tightest passage on the Wild Caving Tour.

title: The Heavenly Room
altText: A park ranger stands as a silhouette in a large room in Jewel Cave.
caption: The Heavenly Room is a large room along the Historic Lantern Tour within the historic entrance of Jewel Cave.

title: Nailhead Spar
altText: White-colored crystals with blunt tips sparkle against a black background.
caption: Calcite crystals, such as nailhead spar, cover most of the walls, ceilings, and floors within Jewel Cave.

title: Rocky Mountain Bighorn Sheep
altText: A bighorn sheep ram stands in the highway, with a camper stopped in the other lane.
caption: Rocky mountain bighorn sheep are commonly seen within the monument, usually along Highway 16.

title: Red Squirrel
altText: A small red squirrel stands on a dead pine branch.
caption: Red squirrels are commonly observed along the Roof Trail near the visitor center.

title: Fall Season Foliage in Fog
altText: Fog filters through a stand of pine trees, with red and yellow foliage on the forest floor.
caption: Autumn is a beautiful time of year to visit the monument. The colors of fall are often the best during mid to late September.

title: Winter in the Pines
altText: Snow covers the ground and flocks pine trees.
caption: Although a "slower" time of year, the winter season offers some of the best scenery of the ponderosa pine forest.

title: Canyons Trail Descending toward the Historic Entrance
altText: A paved path with steps heads down a hillside, with rock overhangs and pine trees nearby.
caption: A portion of the Canyons Trail passes the historic entrance of Jewel Cave, with breathtaking views of nearby Hell Canyon.

title: Theodore Roosevelt Addresses an Audience
altText: A look-alike Theodore Roosevelt speaks to visitors on the patio of the visitor center.
caption: The monument hosts special events each summer, such as living history presentations of President Theodore Roosevelt.

title: The Ranger Cabin at Jewel Cave
altText: A small, dark-brown cabin sits on top of a hill with a walking path leading to its front porch.
caption: The original park ranger cabin, built during the 1930s, is available for public access during the summer and early fall seasons.

title: Visitor Center Exhibit Area
altText: A wall mural showing red-colored rock transitions into a room with a large cave map on the wall.
caption: The visitor center provides several engaging exhibits and displays about the resources within the monument.

title: Pasque Flowers in Bloom

caption: Jewel Cave is home to several species of wildflowers, such as the South Dakota State Flower - the pasque flower.

title: Bats at Jewel Cave
altText: A small brown bat rests on a red-orange colored rock.
caption: Nine species of bats live within Jewel Cave National Monument. At times, visitors may see bats outside the historic entrance.

title: Overview of Kalaupapa Peninsula
altText: View of Kalaupapa Peninsula from Overlook
caption: The Kalaupapa peninsula was once a prison for those inflicted with Hansen's disease.  Approximately 8000 people were forced to live their lives in isolation here.

title: Pali (Sea cliffs) at Kalaupapa
altText: View of pali (sea cliffs) on the north shore of Molokai
caption: Kalaupapa National Historical Park contains some of the world's tallest sea cliffs, which formed a natural barrier to the Hansen's disease settlement.

title: Kalaupapa Settlement
altText: View of residences along Damien Road
caption: Patients lived in a combination of group homes and single family residences at Kalaupapa.

title: Kalawao at Sunrise
altText: View of the sunrise over the north shore of Molokai
caption: Kalawao with its rocky shoreline was the landing spot for the first Hansen's disease patients sent to Kalaupapa.

title: Black Sand Beach
altText: View of Black Sand Beach and Awahua Bay
caption: Black Sand Beach is one of several sandy beaches at Kalaupapa, most known for its sea turtle nesting habitat.

title: Archeological Sites at Kalaupapa
altText: View of rock walls and native Hawaiian archeological sites at Kalaupapa.
caption: Kalaupapa is also home to one of the most well-preserved archeological complexes in all of Hawaii.

title: Siloama Church at Kalawao
altText: Siloama Church at Kalawao settlement
caption: Siloama Church was the first church established at Kalawao in 1866.  It is one of two remaining buildings at Kalawao today.

title: St. Damien's Grave
altText: View of St. Damien's Grave at Kalawao with ocean in background.
caption: Saint Damien is one of two Catholic saints at Kalaupapa who came to serve the Hansen's disease patients in the late 19th century.

title: Paschoal Hall
altText: View of Paschoal Hall also known as the Kalaupapa Social Hall
caption: Paschoal Hall, also known as the Kalaupapa Social Hall, served as the community's recreational center with movies, entertainers, and dances.

title: Papaloa Cemetery
altText: View of grave markers at Papaloa Cemetery in Kalaupapa
caption: Kalaupapa is the final resting place for approximately 8000 people. Papaloa Cemetery is the largest cemetery located along the west shoreline of the peninsula.

title: Kana'ana Hou Church
altText: View of Kana'ana Hou Church through gates
caption: Religion was a prominent part of patients' lives at Kalaupapa. Protestant, Catholic, and Mormon churches were established at both Kalawao and Kalaupapa settlements.

title: Kalaupapa Pier
altText: View of warehouse and store at the Kalaupapa Pier
caption: The Kalaupapa Pier, warehouse, and store were the lifeline of the settlement during the 20th century. Historically, shipments of goods and supplies were sent to Kalaupapa weekly.

title: Kaloko Fishpond
altText: The rehabilitation of Kaloko fishpond started in 1998 and still continues today....
caption: Kaloko Fishpond is the largest walled fishpond in the state (800 ft. long)

title: Ai'opio fishtrap

caption: Ai'opio fishtrap is on the south boundary of the park and is used frequently by visitors and for special events (annual children's cultural festival shown).

caption: The "Old Trace," the historic trail commemorated by the Natchez Trace Parkway today, still survives in segments. The Old Trace was the main road through the Old Southwest, connecting Natchez to Nashville by going through Chickasaw and Choctaw lands.

caption: Cherokee leader John Brown, who owned 640 acres in this area, ordered the construction of Browns Ferry Tavern in 1803. In 1838, the road running past this structure was the route by which several Cherokee detachments were removed to present-day Oklahoma.

title: Grave Stone of William Adair, Stilwell, Oklahoma

caption: Women's Rights NHP celebrates the American Women's Rights Movement and its inception in Seneca Falls, New York.

title: Visitor Center
altText: Front of Women's Rights National Historical Park Visitor Center
caption: Visitors can come to learn about the largest social movement in history, the women's rights movement.

title: First Wave Statue
altText: A bronze statue group of people wearing 19th-century clothing.
caption: The First Wave Statue is one of the iconic sites of Women's Rights NHP.

title: Elizabeth Cady Stanton House
altText: The Elizabeth Cady Stanton House with a tree and National Park Service sign in the front yard
caption: Take a ranger led tour in the home where Elizabeth Cady Stanton led the women's rights movement and raised 7 children

caption: The Belvedere is the conceptual center of the World War I Memorial, providing a commanding view of its major features.

altText: A close up of one of the Lady Freedom markers
caption: One of the two plaques showing a nation answering the call to defend freedom

altText: WWII Memorial panoramic shot
caption: A nation pays honor in the center of the capital city to the greatest generatioin

title: Aerial view of World War II Memorial
altText: Aerial view of stone columns around an oval-shaped pool at the World War II Memorial.
caption: 56 columns represent the 50 states, the District of Columbia, and the U.S. territories at the time of World War II.

title: Icy Bay with Mt. St. Elias
altText: Glaciers loom over the ocean with large snowy mountains rising into blue skies
caption: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park contains a diversity of natural features.  Landscapes unique to the North American continent are common here.

title: Backpackers in Mentasta Mountains
altText: Two backpackers sitting in an alpine meadow with snowy mountains in the background
caption: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park is a backpacker's paradise.  A variety of routes take you into beautiful country, including the Mentasta Mountains.

title: Kennecott Mill Town
altText: Historic, large, red buildings with mountains in the background
caption: Kennecott Mines National Historic Landmark is found within Wrangell-St. Elias National Park.  This early 1900's copper mining operation overcame numerous challenges and found success in the heart of the Alaskan wilderness.

title: Logan Glacier
altText: A large glacier with stripes of different colored rock nestled in between barren mountain slopes.
caption: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park contains the greatest concentration of glaciers in North America.  More than 3000 glaciers covering over three million acres of land are found in the park.

title: Wrangell Mountains
altText: Four large snow covered mountains rise above dense forest into blue skies.
caption: The Wrangell Mountains dominate the view of the northern half of the park.   This range lies entirely within the park and reaches 16,390 ft (4,996 m) in height.

altText: Copper Center Visitor Center
caption: Copper Center Visitor Center

title: First Flight
altText: Orville takes to the air in the flyer for the first time as Wilbur assists.
caption: Orville takes to the air in the flyer for the first time on December 17, 1903.

title: Busts and Moon
altText: Copper busts of the two brothers with the moon visible in the blue sky.
caption: Copper busts of Wilbur and Orville Wright stand atop Big Kill Devil Hill.

title: First Flight Boulder
altText: The large boulder and plaque sit where the Wright brothers first flew in their 1903 flyer.
caption: This boulder marks the spot where the Wright brothers first flew in their 1903 flyer.

title: Walking Along History
altText: Visitors walk along the flight line where Wilbur and Orville Wright flew and landed.
caption: Visitors explore the landing spots of the Wright brothers' first flights.

title: Wright Brothers Monument
altText: View straight up at the monument with the Sun directly behind the stonework.
caption: Black-and-white image of the Wright Brothers Monument

title: National Aviation Day
altText: Two young girls look across the grounds of Wright Brothers National Memorial.
caption: Visitors enjoy celebrating aviation where modern aviation was born.

title: Journey to the Top
altText: Visitors moving along a sidewalk to the top of the hill where the monument stands.
caption: Visitors climb Big Kill Devil Hill to visit the Wright Brothers Monument.

title: Wupatki Pueblo
altText: a large stone pueblo in a desert beneath mostly sunny skies with mountains in background
caption: Wupatki, the monument's namesake pueblo, is made up of more than 100 rooms. The trail also features an ancient ball court and natural blowhole.

title: Wukoki Pueblo
altText: a man and woman walking toward a three-story sandstone tower
caption: Wukoki Pueblo is located three miles from the Wupatki Visitor Center.

title: Lomaki Pueblo
altText: Walls of a white limestone and red sandstone structure under light blue skies with white clouds.
caption: Lomaki means "beautiful house" in the Hopi language. The pueblo is located ten miles from the visitor center and is reachable via a short, 0.5 mile trail.

title: Dark Skies over the Desert
altText: stars and the Milky Way galaxy over a desert juniper tree
caption: Wupatki National Monument features exceptionally dark night skies and hosts numerous star parties each year.

title: Citadel Pueblo
altText: Side view of the Nalakihu and Citadel Pueblos
caption: The Citadel Pueblo sits atop a small cinder hill overlooking the surrounding grasslands.

title: Grand Prismatic Spring
altText: Brilliant blues and greens of a hot spring ringed by oranges, yellows, reds, and browns.
caption: The bright colors found in Grand Prismatic Spring come from thermophiles—microorganisms that thrive in hot temperatures

title: Aurum Geyser
altText: A crowd of people standing along a wooden boardwalk watches a geyser erupt.
caption: Aurum Geyser erupting

title: Black Pool
altText: A visitor stands on a boardwalk near a hot spring and a lake.
caption: Black Pool at the West Thumb Geyser Basin

title: Beehive Geyser
altText: People on a wooden boardwalk watch a geyser erupt.
caption: Visitors to the Upper Geyser Basin watch Beehive Geyser erupt.

title: Wolf howling
altText: A wolf howls while standing on a snowy field.
caption: Alpha male of the Canyon wolf pack

title: Fishing Bridge Trailside Museum
altText: Visitors walk into a rustic, log and stone building.
caption: The stone-and-log architecture of Fishing Bridge Trailside Museum became a prototype for park buildings all around the country

title: Great Fountain Geyser
altText: A geyser erupting in the middle of a large pool.
caption: Great Fountain Geyser erupts against a blue summer sky

title: Old Faithful Geyser in winter
altText: A crowd in front of an erupting geyser during a snowy winter day.
caption: Winter is a magical time to watch Old Faithful Geyser erupt

title: Palette Spring
altText: Visitors walk in front of a brightly colored, terraced landscape.
caption: The vibrant colors of Palette Springs are formed by thermophiles—heat-loving organisms

title: Bighorn sheep
altText: Two bighorn sheep laying on the ground.
caption: Two bighorn rams rest and chew their cud

title: Lower Falls from Artist Point
altText: A river plunges into a steep, barren canyon.

caption: Bison near Mud Volcano

caption: Grizzly bear in the woods

title: The Moore House
altText: The Moore House
caption: The Moore House where the terms of surrender were negotiated.

title: National Cemetery
altText: National Cemetery showing tombstones, American Flag and Cemetery Lodge in sunlight.
caption: Civil War National Cemetery

title: The Nelson House
altText: Nelson house on main street.
caption: The Nelson house on main street of Yorktown.  Home of Thomas Nelson Jr.  a signer of the Declaration of Independence.

title: Yorktown Victory Monument
altText: Yorktown Victory Monument
caption: Yorktown Victory Monument

title: Yorktown Battlefield Earthworks:Redoubt 9
altText: Yorktown Battlefield Earthworks:Redoubt 9

title: Yosemite Falls on a Winter Morning
altText: Two tall waterfalls flowing down snow covered granite walls.
caption: Yosemite Falls will sometimes only trickle at the end of summer, but wet winters can rejuvenate the flow.

title: Upper Yosemite Fall and Merced River in spring
altText: Upper Yosemite Fall and Merced River in spring
caption: Upper Yosemite Fall and Merced River in spring

title: Rainbow over Half Dome
altText: A rainbow over a mountain in the distance.
caption: A hike up to Sentinel Dome rewards people with great views of the landscape around them.

title: Cathedral Peak and Lake in Autumn
altText: A mountain reflecting in a lake.
caption: Cathedral Peak is one of the most recognizable peaks in the Yosemite Wilderness.

title: Lower Yosemite Fall
altText: A waterfall flowing down a granite cliff.
caption: The walk to Lower Yosemite Fall is a popular and easy hike.

title: View of Yosemite Valley in Winter
altText: El Capitan on left, Cathedral Rocks on the right, all covered in snow, low clouds and sun
caption: A rewarding view of Yosemite Valley in winter as seen from Tunnel View.

title: Yosemite Valley from Tunnel View
altText: Glaciated valley with vertical cliffs.
caption: Tunnel View is perhaps one of the most photographed views in the park.

title: Glen Aulin
altText: Mountains reflecting in water
caption: Glen Auilin is one of five High Sierra Camps, located in the Yosemite high country.

title: Giant Sequoia Trees in the Mariposa Grove of Giant Sequoias
altText: Cluster of tall trees with cloudy sky.
caption: Yosemite National Park's massive giant sequoias (Sequoiadendron giganteum) live in three groves in the park. The most famous of these is the Mariposa Grove, which contains about 500 mature giant sequoias.

title: Tenaya Lake at Sunset
altText: Mountains surrounding a lake.
caption: Tenaya Lake is a favorite place to stop along the Tioga Road in summer.

title: Half Dome
altText: Granite dome with trace amounts of snow.
caption: Half Dome is one of the most recognizable granitic formations in the world.

title: El Capitan
altText: El Capitan and reflection in Merced River with some low clouds
caption: El Capitan rises over 3,000 feet above the floor of Yosemite Valley.

title: Yucca House Spring
altText: Cattails with mesa in background.
caption: Cattails define marshy locations watered by springs.

title: View of Mesa Verde from Yucca House
altText: View of Mesa Verde landform
caption: View of Mesa Verde above from Yucca House in the valley below.

title: Walls of the Yucca House Pueblo
altText: Masonry walls seen through shrubbery
caption: Ancient walls of Yucca House still standing today.

title: Ancestral Puebloan Wall at Yucca House
altText: Close up view of an ancient wall at Yucca House
caption: View of an Ancestral Puebloan wall still standing at Yucca House.

title: Yucca House in Context
altText: View of  ancient wall in the center of agricultural land surrounding it.
caption: The Yucca House archeological site is surrounded by agricultural land today.

title: Aerial view of the Yukon River
altText: Aerial view of the Yukon River during fall colors
caption: The Yukon River is a vast body of water that has carved its path into the landscape over millions of years.

title: Coal Creek Dredge
altText: Historic Coal Creek Dredge
caption: The historic Coal Creek Dredge sits nonoperational at its original site, near Slaven's Roadhouse

title: Eagle Visitor Center
altText: Eagle Visitor Center in front of Eagle Bluff
caption: The Eagle Visitor Center sits atop a small bluff overlooking the Yukon River.

title: Slaven's Roadhouse during fall colors
altText: Slaven's Roadhouse during fall colors
caption: A historic roadhouse and public use cabin on the Yukon River

title: Calico Bluff and the Yukon River
altText: Calico Bluff rising above the Yukon River that also winds into the distance behind the bluff
caption: Calico Bluff, showing 4 million years of rock layers, rises above the Yukon River and is one of many bluffs along the 130 miles of river in the preserve

title: The Wild and Scenic Charley River
altText: Aerial view of the Charley River winding through the tundra and mountains
caption: The 105 mile-long Wild and Scenic Charley River's watershed is completely protected within the preserve boundary.

title: The Watchman
altText: A triangular sandstone mountain overlooks green and yellow foliage. A cloudy blue sky is overhead.
caption: The Watchman in Zion Canyon

title: The Towers of the Virgin
altText: The sun sets behind large red and white towers of sandstone.
caption: The Towers of the Virgin in Zion Canyon

title: Zion Canyon Visitor Center
altText: A sandstone and glass building surrounded by tall trees with a mountain behind it.
caption: The Zion Canyon Visitor Center is a great place to stop to learn about the park!

title: The Narrows
altText: Tall, red, sandstone walls enclose a narrow river.
caption: The Narrows can be accessed at the end of Riverside Walk, just follow the river.

title: Kolob Arch
altText: A red sandstone arch under a clear blue sky.
caption: Deep in Zion's Wilderness site Kolob Arch, one of the largest free-standing arches in the world.
